complexity_drivers: Custom LWC components for map integration (Mapbox/ArcGIS 1m GSD), white-label embeddable widgets, Section 508/WCAG Level AA compliance testing, mobile-first responsive design for 48M users, advanced search with multiple filter criteria, Login.gov SAML SSO integration with JIT provisioning
skills_needed: Salesforce Architect, LWC Developer (JavaScript/HTML/CSS), Experience Cloud Specialist, Accessibility Specialist (508 compliance), Integration Developer (SAML), UX Designer, QA Engineer
risks: Map integration complexity (custom LWC for 1m GSD imagery may take longer than 1-2 sprints); white-label widget cross-domain embedding security; Login.gov SSO delays if Government sandbox environment not available; peak load performance (48M annual visitors) requires extensive load testing
solution_approach: Experience Cloud Digital Experiences site with custom LWC components for map (Mapbox/ArcGIS tiles), search UI, and booking flow. Login.gov SAML 2.0 SSO with Apex JIT provisioning handler. White-label widgets use iframe or Web Components with postMessage API for cross-domain communication. Accessibility audit at sprint 12 and sprint 18 (pre-UAT).

complexity_drivers: Role-based access for 5,000 users across 8 agencies with varying permissions (APMs, field staff, concessionaires), offline mobile capability via Salesforce Mobile SDK with local storage and sync conflict resolution, agency-specific dashboards and reporting, business rule configuration UI for non-technical APMs
skills_needed: Salesforce Architect, Experience Cloud Specialist (Partner Community), Mobile Developer (Salesforce Mobile SDK), Apex Developer (sync conflict resolution logic), Report Builder (CRM Analytics), UX Designer
risks: Offline mobile sync conflicts (multiple field users updating same inventory simultaneously); role-based access complexity (8 agencies × multiple user types = permission explosion); Mobile SDK offline validation requires field site pilot with real connectivity constraints
solution_approach: Experience Cloud Partner Community with role-based page layouts and dashboards per agency. Salesforce Mobile SDK with local storage (SQLite) for offline inventory management; sync via Platform Events when connectivity restored. Conflict resolution: last-write-wins for inventory updates; manual Case review for reservation conflicts. Business rule configuration UI: custom LWC wizard with validation.

complexity_drivers: Service Cloud Voice integration with Government-provided toll-free number, Omni-Channel routing configuration for phone/chat/email, case management workflows and SLAs (10am-12am ET, 365 days), Knowledge Base article creation and maintenance, CRM Analytics dashboards for QASP metrics (first-contact resolution, handle time)
skills_needed: Service Cloud Specialist, Omni-Channel Administrator, Knowledge Management Specialist, CRM Analytics Developer, Telephony Integration Engineer (Voice), QA Engineer
risks: Service Cloud Voice setup complexity (requires Amazon Connect or partner telephony provider integration); Omni-Channel routing rules may need tuning during UAT (agent skill matching); Knowledge Base article quality depends on Government content creation
solution_approach: Service Cloud with Omni-Channel routing by skill (reservations, refunds, technical support). Service Cloud Voice integrated with Government toll-free number via Amazon Connect or Salesforce partner telephony. Knowledge Base embedded in Experience Cloud for self-service deflection. CRM Analytics dashboards for QASP surveillance (monthly PMR reporting).

complexity_drivers: 6,000+ facilities each with unique business rules (booking windows: fixed, rolling, sliding freeze; cut-off windows; max stay; seasonal configurations; block release; age/equipment restrictions), complex Apex logic for dynamic booking window calculation and conflict detection, Platform Events for real-time inventory updates across all active sessions, Flow Builder for standard booking workflows, extensive unit testing (85%+ coverage) for rules engine
skills_needed: Salesforce Architect, Senior Apex Developer (complex business logic), Flow Developer, Data Modeler (custom objects: Facility, Site, Season, Business_Rule), QA Engineer (regression testing), Business Analyst (rules translation from incumbent system)
risks: Highest technical risk epic. Business rules complexity may result in logic errors (e.g., double-booking, incorrect booking window calculation); incumbent contractor may not document all rules (reverse-engineering required); performance at scale (10.5M annual reservations) requires Platform Events + batch optimization; phased rollout by agency required to catch edge cases
solution_approach: Custom objects (Facility__c, Site__c, Season__c, Business_Rule__c, Reservation__c) with Master-Detail relationships. Apex classes evaluate business rules dynamically (invoked by Flow during booking workflow). Platform Events broadcast inventory changes to all active user sessions (real-time availability updates). Scheduled batch Apex for nightly inventory recalculation (rolling release, block release). Extensive Apex unit tests (85%+ coverage) with edge case scenarios.

complexity_drivers: Worldpay REST API integration with OAuth 2.0 tokenization (PCI DSS compliance), US Bank Lockbox file-based reconciliation (SFTP + CSV parsing), EMV terminal third-party vendor API integration, Shield Platform Encryption for payment tokens, automated financial reconciliation (match transactions to bank data), exception handling and Case creation for unmatched transactions, chargeback processing workflow
skills_needed: Salesforce Architect, Integration Developer (REST APIs, SFTP), Apex Developer (batch reconciliation jobs), Security Specialist (PCI DSS, Shield Encryption), Financial Systems Analyst (reconciliation logic), QA Engineer
risks: PCI DSS compliance gaps (tokenization must be correctly implemented; annual SAQ A-EP self-assessment required); Worldpay API documentation may be incomplete (validate early); US Bank Lockbox file format changes (error handling required); EMV terminal vendor API may not exist (fallback: manual transaction logging); financial reconciliation logic complexity (multiple payment methods, refunds, chargebacks)
solution_approach: Worldpay tokenization: LWC iframe collects payment details (Worldpay-hosted), returns token to Salesforce; Apex calls Worldpay charge API with token. US Bank Lockbox: Scheduled Apex batch (daily 6am ET) downloads CSV via SFTP, parses, matches to Reservation__c by Confirmation_Number__c. Unmatched transactions → Case creation. Shield Platform Encryption for Worldpay_Token__c field. Payment_Transaction__c custom object. EMV vendor API → custom Apex REST endpoint for transaction logging.

complexity_drivers: Data Cloud connector setup for 8 agency CMS (S3, Azure, REST API), data prep recipes to harmonize multi-agency schemas, RIDB_Entry__c custom object for non-reservable data, Data Cloud Connect REST API for public bulk CSV downloads, searchable interface in Experience Cloud (map + list views), data quality validation and exception handling
skills_needed: Data Cloud Specialist, ETL Developer (data prep recipes), Salesforce Architect, API Developer (REST endpoints), LWC Developer (search UI), QA Engineer
risks: Agency CMS data quality varies (missing fields, invalid geolocations); data schema changes over time (requires recipe updates); Data Cloud learning curve (newer Salesforce product); public API performance at scale (bulk downloads by external developers)
solution_approach: Data Cloud connectors (S3, Azure, REST) ingest RIDB data from 8 agencies on scheduled basis (hourly/daily per agency). Data prep recipes map agency-specific schemas to unified RIDB schema (Facility_Name, Geolocation, Entry_Type). Data Cloud writes to RIDB_Entry__c object. Experience Cloud search UI queries RIDB_Entry__c + displays on map (Mapbox/ArcGIS). Data Cloud Connect REST API exposes bulk CSV downloads (tokenized access). Data quality validation: quarantine invalid records, create Case for agency correction.

complexity_drivers: USDA ATO application and approval process (6-9 months), Login.gov SAML 2.0 SSO integration, MFA enforcement for government users (agency IdP integration), Shield Platform Encryption for PII (customer profiles, payment tokens), Platform Event Monitoring for audit trails and fraud detection (97% review within 10 business days), role-based access controls (profiles, permission sets, sharing rules for 8 agencies), Zero Trust architecture configuration
skills_needed: Salesforce Architect, Security Specialist (FedRAMP, ATO, PCI DSS), Integration Developer (SAML SSO), Shield Specialist (Platform Encryption, Event Monitoring), Compliance Analyst (NIST 800-53 controls mapping), Government Liaison (USDA CISO coordination)
risks: Highest schedule risk epic. ATO delays are common (6-9 months typical, but can extend to 12+ months if USDA CISO backlog); Login.gov SSO sandbox environment may not be available early in project; Shield Event Monitoring configuration complexity (define fraud detection thresholds); role-based access for 8 agencies requires extensive testing (permission explosion)
solution_approach: Government Cloud org setup (FedRAMP Moderate pre-authorized). USDA ATO application (Month 1-6; CLIN 10008 labor for security plan documentation). Login.gov SAML 2.0 SSO with Apex JIT provisioning handler. Agency IdP SAML/OAuth integration for government users (MFA enforced). Shield Platform Encryption for PII fields (Worldpay_Token__c, SSN__c, Email__c, Date_of_Birth__c). Platform Event Monitoring tracks login anomalies, bulk exports, permission changes → Case creation for fraud review. Role-based access: 6 profiles, 4 permission sets, criteria-based sharing rules per agency.

complexity_drivers: 21M+ historical reservation records migration, 6,000+ facility inventory definitions with business rules translation from incumbent system, 10M+ user profile migration (Person Accounts), incumbent contractor cooperation uncertainty (may not document business rules fully), ETL tool setup (MuleSoft or Heroku) for complex transformations, phased migration strategy (Inventory → Active Reservations → Historical → User Profiles), parallel operations period (2-4 weeks) for validation, data quality checkpoints and rollback plans per phase
skills_needed: Data Migration Lead, ETL Developer (MuleSoft or Heroku), Salesforce Architect, Data Quality Analyst, Business Analyst (business rules reverse-engineering), Apex Developer (data transformation scripts), QA Engineer (data validation), Transition Manager (incumbent coordination)
risks: Highest data risk epic. Incumbent contractor may not fully cooperate (SOO Section 6.3 obligations unclear); business rules reverse-engineering may take longer than expected (sample rules provided in RFI are incomplete); data quality issues (duplicate accounts, missing required fields, invalid geolocations) may require extensive cleansing; 21M+ record volume requires Bulk API optimization (avoid governor limits); parallel operations period critical for catching data integrity issues before cutover
solution_approach: Phased migration: (1) Inventory (Facility__c, Site__c, Season__c, Business_Rule__c) → validate with APMs; (2) Active Reservations (next 12 months) → validate with field users; (3) Historical Reservations (prior 24 months) → validate with PMO; (4) User Profiles (Account Person Accounts) → validate with sample users. ETL tool (MuleSoft Anypoint or Heroku) for business rule translation and data cleansing. Data Loader for straightforward migrations. Parallel operations (2-4 weeks): Incumbent system + Salesforce both active; compare reservation counts, revenue, user activity. Rollback plan per phase. Data quality checkpoints: automated validation rules + manual spot checks by APMs.

complexity_drivers: CRM Analytics (Tableau) embedded dashboards in Experience Cloud (PMO, APM, public-facing), standard QASP reports (monthly performance metrics), ad-hoc query builder for APMs, integration with Service Cloud (case metrics), payment systems (financial reconciliation), and Data Cloud (RIDB analytics), dashboard design and iteration with stakeholders
skills_needed: CRM Analytics Developer (Tableau), Report Builder, Salesforce Architect, Data Modeler (fact tables, dimensions), UX Designer (dashboard layouts), Business Analyst (QASP metrics requirements)
risks: Dashboard requirements may evolve during UAT (stakeholder feedback); CRM Analytics licensing cost scales with user count (5,000 internal users + public-facing dashboards); ad-hoc query builder requires training for APMs (non-technical users)
solution_approach: CRM Analytics (Tableau) dashboards embedded in Experience Cloud Partner Community (APM view) and public portal (popular facilities, availability trends). Standard reports: QASP metrics (uptime, first-contact resolution, fraud detection rate, revenue by agency). Ad-hoc query builder: Tableau self-service for APMs (drag-and-drop interface). Integration: CRM Analytics connects to Case (Service Cloud), Payment_Transaction__c (financial data), RIDB_Entry__c (Data Cloud). Dashboard design: iterative workshops with PMO, APMs (Months 9-12).

complexity_drivers: Marketing Cloud may be out of MVP scope (flagged as optional); if included: broadcast messaging (SMS, push, email), Marketing Cloud integration with Salesforce Platform (Journey Builder), customer segmentation (frequent visitors, lapsed users), A/B testing for conversion optimization, annual outreach strategy development
skills_needed: Marketing Cloud Specialist, Journey Builder Developer, Marketing Analyst (segmentation, A/B testing), Copywriter (campaign content), Salesforce Architect (Platform integration)
risks: Low priority for MVP; likely deferred to post-MVP Phase 2 (Years 2-10); if included in MVP, Marketing Cloud licensing cost is significant ($40K+ annual); integration with Platform requires Marketing Cloud Connect (additional config); stakeholder prioritization unclear (OQ-07 in Architecture Reference)
solution_approach: If in scope: Marketing Cloud with Journey Builder for automated campaigns ('Your favorite campground just opened reservations for summer 2027'). SMS (MobileConnect), push notifications (MobilePush), email (Email Studio). Marketing Cloud Connect syncs Account (Person Account) data from Salesforce Platform. Customer segmentation: frequent visitors (>5 reservations/year), lapsed users (no reservation in 12 months), high-value users (annual pass holders). A/B testing for email subject lines, send times. If out of scope: Experience Cloud native email templates + in-app notifications suffice for MVP.

complexity_drivers: Multi-tiered training programs (government users: APMs, field staff, concessionaires; public users: self-service KB), Salesforce DevOps Center setup (Git-backed deployment pipeline), Agile program management (sprint planning, UAT coordination, quarterly PMRs, QASP surveillance), system documentation (architecture diagrams, code annotations, user guides), train-the-trainer for government users
skills_needed: Training Specialist (curriculum development, train-the-trainer), Technical Writer (system documentation), DevOps Engineer (DevOps Center setup, GitHub integration), Scrum Master (Agile ceremonies), PMO Liaison (QASP reporting, quarterly PMRs)
risks: Training effectiveness depends on Government user engagement (5,000 stakeholders); DevOps Center learning curve for Government IT staff (if they will manage post-go-live); documentation maintenance (must be updated as system evolves); QASP metrics reporting requires automated dashboards (dependency on E09 Analytics)
solution_approach: Training: (1) Government users: role-based hands-on workshops (APM: business rule configuration, field user: offline mobile workflows, concessionaire: reservation management); (2) Public users: self-service Knowledge Base articles embedded in Experience Cloud. Train-the-trainer: certify 2-3 government 'champions' per agency to support peers post-go-live. DevOps Center: GitHub repository setup, Work Item workflow configuration, deployment pipeline (Dev → Integration → UAT → Prod). System documentation: architecture diagrams (Lucidchart), code annotations (Apex comments), user guides (PDF + Knowledge articles). Agile: bi-weekly sprint planning, demos, retrospectives; monthly stakeholder reviews; quarterly PMRs (QASP reporting).
